rules_fired.because: High vapor pressure (≥5 kPa at 20°C) causes significant evaporation inside the tip. Without pre-wetting, the headspace is dry air; the solvent flash-evaporates into it, creating a vapour cushion that compresses and releases unpredictably, drifting dispensed volume by 5–15%. Three aspirate-expel cycles saturate the headspace before the actual dispensing step. Fast dispense minimises dwell-time in the tip.
rules_fired.cite: Eppendorf Application Note: Pipetting Volatile Liquids, 2018. NIST WebBook CAS 64-17-5 (vapor pressure 5.95 kPa at 20°C).

rules_fired.because: The SDS (Section 7 or 8) specifies ventilation controls or fume hood use for this reagent. This category spans volatile toxic solvents (pyridine, DMF, heptane), cytotoxic and genotoxic compounds (docetaxel, streptozocin, azoxymethane), and reagents whose vapours or aerosols pose an inhalation hazard. A chemical fume hood provides the primary engineering control — never handle these reagents on the open bench.
rules_fired.cite: OSHA Laboratory Standard 29 CFR 1910.1450. Manufacturer SDS Section 7/8.
